title: Tapping to Picture My Family's Future Freedom
subtitle: A tapping practice for grounding a vision of generational freedom as something real and buildable
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 71 of a 12-week money programme. The script targets the doubt that a generational wealth vision is too big to hold or to build, and moves toward a settled trust in that picture as a foundation worth building from.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 71 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the idea of building wealth for future generations feels too large or abstract to hold as a real working vision.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 71 of a 12-week money programme. Week 11 theme: generational wealth vision. The script targets the doubt that a legacy vision is too large to hold as a real, working picture, and moves toward a settled trust in that picture as a foundation worth building from.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once found it hard to picture a wealthy family future, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though part of me wondered if that vision was too big, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I sometimes doubt I can create it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I see generations living free from money stress.
Side of eye: Children and grandchildren with choices and ease.
Under eye: A family life built on abundance.
Under nose: A long line of security and joy.
Chin: I hold that picture clearly.
Collarbone: I love imagining their freedom.
Under arm: I feel excited by this future.
Top of head: I let this vision guide me.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I create a wealthy, secure family line.
Side of eye: I pass down opportunity and wisdom.
Under eye: I see abundance as their everyday life.
Under nose: I love building freedom for generations.
Chin: I trust myself to lay a strong foundation.
Collarbone: I feel proud to lead this legacy.
Under arm: I welcome every resource to make it real.
Top of head: I hold and honour this future family vision.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 71 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
